hints.text: **Low-cam** (bigger car blur, roads look wide, low angle) — only Japan & Liechtenstein are also always low-cam
Plates **fully white, no blue strip**; **very short front plate**; rear has red country emblem left + canton emblem right
Bollards black-and-white (white/grey reflector), wedge-shaped or cylindrical w/ rounded top
Directional-sign colour = road type: **blue = main road, white = side road**
Pedestrian sign = **7 stripes** in a **tall blue rectangle** (almost unique in Europe)
**Long dashed yellow** road lines; **yellow-painted pedestrian crossings** (only CH + Liechtenstein in Europe)
Road signs with a **round grey metal border**; town-entry signs white/blue w/ metal frame + canton abbreviation
Chevrons black w/ white arrow (Austria = red/white or red/yellow)
**South extremely mountainous** (thin valleys); north = farmland on flat/rolling hills w/ forested hills in distance
Alpine architecture: light warm stone + wood, gable tiled roofs, **two-part wooden shutters**; unique thick-metal-frame bus-stop signs
**4 official languages** (German/French/Italian/Romansh); Swiss German has **no ß**; speed-sign text varies by region
**Canton 2-letter abbreviation** on town signs & plates; postal-code first digits increase **west→east**
**Jura Mountains** (NW) = fairly flat + a long chain of forested ridges, exposed limestone; rock walls
**Ticino** = Italian architecture (south); Graubünden painted-detail stone houses + **Bernina Express** train coverage; Valais thick wooden bollards
